text: The surgeon places a laparoscopic adjustable gastric band, a silicone ring, around the upper portion of the stomach, forming a small pouch above the band.

The surgeon identifies the area at the top portion of the stomach where the laparoscopic adjustable gastric band will be placed. The presence of a hiatal hernia is noted, which will be repaired during the procedure to prevent postoperative reflux.
The surgeon creates a small tunnel behind the top portion of the stomach or esophagus, preparing the site for the gastric band placement. This is performed under direct laparoscopic vision.

A special band passing instrument is maneuvered around the stomach, allowing the laparoscopic adjustable gastric band to be placed within the abdominal cavity. The band is attached to the instrument for safe positioning around the stomach.
The surgeon positions the laparoscopic adjustable gastric band around the top portion of the stomach, forming a small pouch above the band, approximately fifteen to twenty cc in size.

Inside the abdominal cavity, the surgeon assembles the gastric band, securing it in place by sewing a portion of the stomach over the band to the pouch above it. The band consists of a series of balloons, which cannot be sutured directly, necessitating this technique to maintain its position.

The surgeon places several sutures to secure the band around the stomach, preventing it from slipping or moving. After securing the band, it is buckled, and the surrounding area is inspected for safety and proper placement.
The surgeon brings the tubing out through one of the trocar sites and secures it to the abdominal wall, attaching it to a port for future band adjustments.
